sources_used: Hollywood Reporter - 34 Times the Fox Show Forecasted the Future
Snopes - Simpson Predictions That Were Just Internet Hoaxes

predictions.prediction_text: Donald Trump becomes President of the United States
predictions.actual_event: Trump elected 45th President 2016, re-elected 47th in 2024
predictions.notes: Written during Trump's 2000 Reform Party exploratory run. Writer Dan Greaney confirmed satirical intent. A background poster in the episode even showed '2024' next to Trump's image.

predictions.prediction_text: Lisa Simpson's purple pantsuit and white pearls mirrors Kamala Harris at the 2021 inauguration
predictions.actual_event: Kamala Harris wore purple pantsuit and pearls at Biden's 2021 inauguration
predictions.notes: Writer Mike Scully: 'The pantsuit may have been a nod to Hillary Clinton at the time.' Pearls are Lisa's standard outfit. Color was random per animators.

predictions.episode_title: When You Dish Upon a Star
predictions.prediction_text: Disney acquires 20th Century Fox
predictions.actual_event: Disney completed $71.3B acquisition of 21st Century Fox assets in March 2019
predictions.notes: Sign reads '20th Century Fox, A Division of Walt Disney Co.' as a background gag. One of the most specific and verifiable predictions in the dataset.

predictions.prediction_text: Washington Redskins win Super Bowl XXVI over the Buffalo Bills
predictions.actual_event: Washington Redskins defeated Buffalo Bills 37-24 in Super Bowl XXVI
predictions.notes: Episode aired days before the game. Reruns were re-dubbed for subsequent Super Bowls: Cowboys 1993 (correct), 49ers over Chargers 1995 (correct). Multiple hits.

predictions.prediction_text: Siegfried and Roy are attacked by their white tiger during a live performance
predictions.actual_event: Roy Horn mauled by white Bengal tiger Montecore on stage, October 3, 2003
predictions.notes: Show's parody duo Gunter and Ernst mirrors the real 2003 incident closely. Wild animal attacks are a known risk in such acts.

predictions.prediction_text: Smartwatches with screens and voice recognition become mainstream
predictions.actual_event: Samsung Gear launched 2013, Apple Watch 2015. Snopes notes wrist communicators predate Simpsons in many works of fiction.
predictions.notes: Predicted for 2010; arrived 2013-15. Snopes: 'technologically advanced watches have appeared in numerous works of fiction, decades before The Simpsons.' Still a notable hit.

predictions.prediction_text: Video calling via screens becomes standard communication
predictions.actual_event: Apple FaceTime launched June 2010 — exact predicted year
predictions.notes: The 2010 timeframe was spot on for FaceTime. Skype existed earlier (2003) but mainstream video calling took off exactly when predicted.

predictions.prediction_text: The Shard skyscraper built near Big Ben on the London skyline
predictions.actual_event: The Shard completed 2012, matching position and shape shown in background shot
predictions.notes: A pointed spire visible on the left of Big Ben in a background shot. The Shard's position and tapered shape are a striking match.

predictions.episode_title: You Don't Have to Live Like a Referee
predictions.prediction_text: FIFA officials arrested on corruption and bribery charges
predictions.actual_event: 14 FIFA officials indicted by U.S. Dept of Justice, May 2015. Multiple arrests globally.
predictions.notes: Episode also correctly predicted Germany defeating Brazil in the same year's World Cup.

predictions.episode_title: You Don't Have to Live Like a Referee
predictions.prediction_text: Germany defeats Brazil in the 2014 World Cup
predictions.actual_event: Germany defeated Brazil 7-1 in the 2014 World Cup semi-final and won the tournament
predictions.notes: Double prediction in one episode. Homer referees and calls the game fairly — Germany wins.

predictions.prediction_text: Bengt Holmström wins the Nobel Prize in Economics — named specifically
predictions.actual_event: Bengt Holmström won the Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences, October 2016
predictions.notes: Named by name on a freeze-frame betting card. Holmström was a respected MIT economist but the specific Nobel call 6 years out is remarkable.

predictions.prediction_text: U.S. Olympic curling team beats Sweden to win gold medal
predictions.actual_event: U.S. men's curling team defeated Sweden 10-7 at 2018 Winter Olympics in Pyeongchang
predictions.notes: Got both the exact opponent (Sweden) AND the underdog result correct. 8 years in advance. One of the strongest specificity hits in the dataset.

predictions.prediction_text: A viral flu originating in east Asia spreads globally via shipped goods, causing widespread panic
predictions.actual_event: COVID-19 originated in Wuhan, China and caused a global pandemic in 2020
predictions.notes: Snopes: this is 'commenting on past events, not predicting future ones.' Asian flu pandemics (Spanish Flu, Hong Kong Flu, Asian Flu) are a recurring historical pattern. Writer Bill Oakley explicitly said the comparison is coincidental.

predictions.prediction_text: Homer's chalkboard equation approximates the mass of the Higgs boson particle
predictions.actual_event: Higgs boson confirmed by CERN physicists in July 2012
predictions.notes: Dr. Simon Singh verified the equation. Writer David X. Cohen had a physics background. The mass was already theoretically hypothesized — this was an educated extrapolation.

predictions.prediction_text: Cypress Hill performs with the London Symphony Orchestra
predictions.actual_event: Cypress Hill performed with the LSO at Royal Albert Hall, July 10, 2024
predictions.notes: The joke was that someone ordered the LSO 'possibly while high.' B-Real confirmed the Simpsons episode inspired the real performance.

predictions.episode_title: The Ziff Who Came to Dinner
predictions.prediction_text: A fourth Matrix film released around the Christmas holiday
predictions.actual_event: The Matrix Resurrections released December 22, 2021 — Christmas week
predictions.notes: Background poster shows 'Matrix 4' with a Christmas premiere date. Released exactly Christmas week, 17 years later.

predictions.prediction_text: A real minor league baseball team named the Albuquerque Isotopes is established
predictions.actual_event: Calgary Cannons relocated to Albuquerque and renamed the Isotopes in 2003, inspired by fan demand from the episode
predictions.notes: Self-fulfilling prophecy — the real team deliberately named themselves after the Simpsons episode.

predictions.prediction_text: An underwater submersible carrying passengers gets stuck and runs out of oxygen
predictions.actual_event: OceanGate Titan submersible imploded June 2023, killing all 5 passengers
predictions.notes: Homer in a personal submersible loses oxygen and consciousness. Loose parallel — submarine disasters are a recurring theme in fiction generally.

predictions.prediction_text: A voting machine records a vote for one presidential candidate as a vote for another
predictions.actual_event: Viral 2012 video showed Pennsylvania voting machine switching Obama vote to Romney
predictions.notes: Homer tries to vote Obama, machine registers McCain. Nearly identical real-world incident documented in 2012.

predictions.prediction_text: Thieves steal used cooking grease from restaurants to sell for cash
predictions.actual_event: Reports of cooking grease theft across NYC for resale as biodiesel emerged in 2013
predictions.notes: Homer's get-rich-quick scheme involving siphoning restaurant grease became a real criminal enterprise.

predictions.prediction_text: Ringo Starr finally responds to decades-old fan mail
predictions.actual_event: Paul McCartney (not Ringo) replied to two Essex women's fan letter from 50 years earlier in 2013
predictions.notes: Wrong Beatle, but the concept of a Beatle belatedly replying to vintage fan mail came true — transferred to McCartney.

predictions.prediction_text: NASA sends an ordinary civilian to space to boost public interest
predictions.actual_event: UK ran 'Astronaut Wanted' competition 2013; space tourism via Blue Origin/Virgin Galactic expanded 2021+
predictions.notes: More broadly: civilian space travel became real via Bezos, Branson, Musk. NASA itself flew journalist on ISS in 2024.

predictions.prediction_text: Lady Gaga performs at the Super Bowl halftime show descending from wires
predictions.actual_event: Lady Gaga performed Super Bowl LI halftime 2017, descending from roof via harness
predictions.notes: Snopes rated this MIXED. The show depicted Gaga performing suspended but not at the Super Bowl. Some circulated images were also doctored.

predictions.episode_title: Brother Can You Spare Two Dimes
predictions.prediction_text: An app translates a baby's cries into understandable needs
predictions.actual_event: 'Cry Translator' app released analyzing baby cries; multiple similar apps followed
predictions.notes: Herb Powell's baby translator device became a real app category. Surprisingly specific given the year.

predictions.prediction_text: Horse meat found in mainstream beef food products
predictions.actual_event: European horse meat scandal 2013 — horse DNA found in Findus beef lasagna and dozens of products across UK/EU
predictions.notes: Lunchlady Doris adds horse parts to school lunch. The 2013 scandal was one of Europe's largest food fraud events.

predictions.prediction_text: A customer successfully sues an all-you-can-eat restaurant for false advertising after being removed
predictions.actual_event: Multiple real cases in 2012 and 2017 including one in Springfield, Massachusetts
predictions.notes: Homer sues a seafood restaurant for false advertising after being ejected. Real lawsuits followed the same logic — including in a real Springfield.

predictions.prediction_text: Nuclear radiation causes mutant hybrid vegetables and fruit near a power plant
predictions.actual_event: Deformed fruits and vegetables documented near Fukushima Daiichi nuclear plant after 2011 disaster
predictions.notes: Homer creates 'tomacco.' Fukushima fallout produced documented agricultural mutations post-2011.

predictions.episode_title: Two Cars in Every Garage and Three Eyes on Every Fish
predictions.prediction_text: A three-eyed fish is discovered near a nuclear power plant
predictions.actual_event: A three-eyed wolf fish caught near nuclear plant in Córdoba, Argentina in 2011
predictions.notes: Blinky the three-eyed fish — exact scenario. The fish species and deformity type differed, but nuclear plant + three-eyed fish is a direct hit.

predictions.episode_title: Papa's Got a Brand New Badge
predictions.prediction_text: Sellers pass off ferrets as toy poodles by grooming them to look like dogs
predictions.actual_event: Argentine man bought 'toy poodles' at market that turned out to be groomed ferrets on steroids, 2013
predictions.notes: Fat Tony's scam involved cotton-balled ferrets sold as poodles. Eerily specific — and happened in Argentina of all places.

predictions.prediction_text: Canada legalizes recreational marijuana
predictions.actual_event: Canada legalized recreational marijuana October 17, 2018
predictions.notes: Homer and friends cross into Canada to buy cheap drugs. The episode implied Canadian drug policy was liberalizing — correct direction.

predictions.prediction_text: JCPenney files for bankruptcy
predictions.actual_event: JCPenney filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy May 15, 2020 during COVID-19
predictions.notes: Marge walks past a derelict JCPenney in a Springfield mall. Department store decline was an observable trend by 2006.

predictions.prediction_text: Elon Musk acquires Twitter and transforms it
predictions.actual_event: Musk acquired Twitter for $44B in October 2022, rebranded to X
predictions.notes: Episode aired same month as the acquisition — more real-time commentary than prediction.

predictions.prediction_text: Greece defaults on its international debt obligations
predictions.actual_event: Greece became first developed nation to default to IMF, June 2015
predictions.notes: News ticker reads 'Europe puts Greece on eBay.' Greek debt problems were visible even in 2004 — perceptive satire.

predictions.prediction_text: Autocorrect on digital devices produces absurd misinterpretations
predictions.actual_event: iPhone autocorrect errors became a massive cultural phenomenon post-2007
predictions.notes: Snopes notes autocorrect already existed in 1993 (Microsoft Word). The show was satirizing the Apple Newton's notoriously bad recognition. Not a clean prediction.

predictions.prediction_text: An annual event is held to cull invasive snake populations
predictions.actual_event: Florida Python Challenge — annual event to hunt invasive Burmese pythons in the Everglades
predictions.notes: Whacking Day involves killing snakes. Florida's Python Challenge is a real government-backed annual event with thousands of participants.

predictions.prediction_text: Scientists revisit the theory that the universe is shaped like a donut (torus)
predictions.actual_event: Doughnut-shaped universe theory resurged in Nature (2008) and subsequent cosmology papers
predictions.notes: Stephen Hawking tells Homer 'Your theory of a doughnut-shaped universe is intriguing.' The torus universe model had existed since the '80s but gained renewed academic interest post-2008.

predictions.prediction_text: A food compound found in yoga mats is discovered in mainstream bread products
predictions.actual_event: Azodicarbonamide found in Subway bread in 2014 — same compound used in yoga mats
predictions.notes: Lunchlady Doris uses gym mats as food ingredients. The 2014 Subway scandal was almost a direct echo of this joke.

predictions.prediction_text: Ebola virus causes a major global outbreak
predictions.actual_event: Largest recorded Ebola outbreak in history, West Africa 2014-2016, 28,000+ cases
predictions.notes: Marge reads Bart 'Curious George and the Ebola Virus.' The disease existed before 1993 — this is more a reference than a prediction. Low confidence.

predictions.prediction_text: A private company targets 2026 for Mars colonization
predictions.actual_event: SpaceX's 2026 Mars target was not met. Cargo missions now targeting 2026-28, human missions 2030+.
predictions.notes: Parody of Mars One (which went bankrupt in 2019). Direction correct (private Mars missions), but 2026 target definitively missed.

predictions.prediction_text: AI and automation cause mass displacement of human workers
predictions.actual_event: Accelerating in 2024-26 — Google, Microsoft, Salesforce AI layoffs documented
predictions.notes: Mr. Burns replaces plant workers with robots. US Senate report estimates AI could displace ~100M jobs over the next decade. Actively unfolding.

predictions.prediction_text: The first female President of the United States
predictions.notes: Lisa becomes president in the episode. The Trump prediction came true; the female president half has not yet. Kamala Harris came closest in 2024.

predictions.prediction_text: U.S. government officially acknowledges the existence of extraterrestrial life
predictions.notes: Fans in 2026 citing ongoing UAP congressional hearings and Pentagon reports. Very speculative stretch.

predictions.prediction_text: Robotic/automated systems malfunction and turn hostile toward humans
predictions.notes: Animatronic park robots attack visitors. Very vague. Fans connecting to AI safety concerns in 2025-26 is a stretch.

predictions.prediction_text: Holographic display technology enters mainstream consumer use
predictions.notes: Bart receives a holographic message. Limited commercial holograms exist but are far from mainstream.

predictions.prediction_text: A three-eyed fish is caught near a nuclear plant
predictions.actual_event: A three-eyed wolf fish caught near nuclear plant in Córdoba, Argentina in 2011
predictions.notes: Note: this is the same event as id:28. Keeping both episode references as two episodes covered the Blinky concept.

predictions.prediction_text: Killer insects (murder hornets) make headlines during a period of disease outbreak
predictions.actual_event: Asian Giant Hornets ('murder hornets') headlines spring 2020 during COVID lockdowns
predictions.notes: Timing overlap is coincidental — the hornets were already present in the Pacific Northwest. Very weak specificity.

predictions.prediction_text: Face transplant surgery becomes a successful medical procedure
predictions.actual_event: First full face transplant completed 2010; over 40 cases by 2020
predictions.notes: Sideshow Bob swaps faces with Walt Warren. First full face transplant actually happened the same year the episode aired.

predictions.episode_title: The City of New York vs. Homer Simpson
predictions.prediction_text: Simpsons predicted the 9/11 attacks via a '$9' brochure next to the Twin Towers image
predictions.actual_event: September 11 attacks, 2001
predictions.notes: DEBUNKED — Snopes: 'The scene lacked any narrative elements that could be seen as a prediction.' Requires interpreting '$9' + Twin Towers as '9/11.' Classic confirmation bias. Al Jean: 'The 9/11 one is so bizarre.'

predictions.prediction_text: The Simpsons predicted the Silicon Valley Bank collapse (doctored clip)
predictions.actual_event: Silicon Valley Bank collapsed March 2023
predictions.notes: DEBUNKED — Snopes confirmed the clip was doctored. Original episode shows 'First Bank of Springfield,' not 'Silicon Valley Bank.' Scene parodies 'It's a Wonderful Life.'

predictions.prediction_text: The Simpsons predicted Trump touching a glowing orb with Saudi leaders
predictions.actual_event: Trump photographed touching illuminated globe with Saudi King and Egyptian President, May 2017
predictions.notes: DEBUNKED — Snopes confirmed the Simpsons-style image first appeared May 26, 2017 — six days AFTER the real photo. It was created in response to the photo, not before it.

predictions.prediction_text: The Simpsons predicted the Beirut explosion
predictions.actual_event: Beirut port explosion August 4, 2020, killing 218 people
predictions.notes: DEBUNKED — Snopes confirmed the viral clip was spliced together from two unrelated episodes. Both scenes featured explosions but had nothing to do with each other or Beirut.

predictions.prediction_text: The Simpsons predicted the exact date of Pope Francis's death in 2025
predictions.actual_event: Pope Francis died April 21, 2025
predictions.notes: DEBUNKED — Snopes confirmed the viral clip showed AI-generated Simpsons imagery with visual tells (missing pupils, garbled text, synthetic narration). No real episode contained this prediction.

predictions.prediction_text: The Simpsons predicted the Apple Vision Pro headset
predictions.actual_event: Apple Vision Pro released February 2024
predictions.notes: DEBUNKED — Snopes: the episode was satirizing VR headsets that already existed in 2016 (Oculus Rift). Not a prediction of Apple's specific product — just commentary on existing VR tech.

predictions.episode_title: The Man Who Came to Be Dinner
predictions.prediction_text: The Simpsons predicted the Coldplay kiss cam scandal (ColdplayGate)
predictions.actual_event: Coldplay concert kiss cam caught Astronomer CEO with alleged affair partner, July 2025
predictions.notes: DEBUNKED — Snopes July 2025: reviewed actual episodes and found zero moments that could be classified as a prediction. Claimed episode either didn't exist or was unrelated.

predictions.episode_title: How I Spent My Strummer Vacation
predictions.prediction_text: Rolling Stones still touring in old age ('Steel Wheelchair Tour')
predictions.actual_event: The Rolling Stones continued touring into the 2020s; Mick Jagger performing at 80+
predictions.notes: A poster in Lisa's Wedding (S06E19) reads 'Rolling Stones Steel Wheelchair Tour 2010.' The Stones were still actively touring well into the 2020s — the joke landed.
